title: rubus serpens var. calyculatus
intro: Rubus serpens var. calyculatus, also known as the creeping blackberry or thimbleberry, is a type of bramble plant that belongs to the rose family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds and reducing pain. The health benefits of Rubus serpens var. calyculatus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and provide antioxidant properties that help protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
